scope.summary: Add lane recovery, orphan detection, replay, and diagnostics state.
forbidden_paths: best-effort recovery based on terminal text only
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: A ModelLaneRun can be reconstructed from canonical run, lane, message, artifact, and status records.
Orphaned subagent lanes are detected and marked with CX-MM-009.
Missing payload refs produce CX-MM-006 with recovery hints.
Replay does not require chat history.

ModelLaneRun recovery MUST persist lane checkpoints containing run_id, lane_id, session_id/model_session_id, status, last_event_ledger_seq, last_message_id, open payload refs, lease_id, idempotency scope, and recovery_state. Replay MUST load the latest checkpoint, apply EventLedger records after that sequence in order, ignore duplicate idempotency keys, and emit typed failure if checkpoint or replay order is missing/corrupt.

MT-007 MUST define and test canonical EventLedger/Flight Recorder event families for RUN_CREATED/RUN_COMPLETED/RUN_FAILED, LANE_PLANNED/LANE_STARTED/LANE_STATUS_CHANGED/LANE_COMPLETED/LANE_FAILED/LANE_CANCELLED/ORPHAN_DETECTED, MESSAGE_RECORDED/PAYLOAD_REF_RECORDED/PAYLOAD_REF_MISSING, RECOVERY_REQUESTED/REPLAY_RECONSTRUCTED/RECOVERY_FAILED. Every family row MUST include run_id, lane_id where applicable, session_id/model_session_id, trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id or span links, wp_id, mt_id, error code, artifact refs, and replay hint.

ModelLane recovery MUST persist and inspect lane leases with lease_id, scope_kind, scope_ref, holder_actor_id, holder_session_id, lease_expires_at_utc, takeover_policy, and release/cancel/failure state. Orphan recovery MUST distinguish active lease, expired lease, cancelled lane, crashed lane, and never-started lane, and must emit durable typed denial/reclaim receipts for takeover or unrecoverable CX-MM-009 paths.

Replay reconstructs run/lane/message/artifact/status/checkpoint state from PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT records without provider trace history, terminal scrollback, UI rows, Flight Recorder rows, or chat history.

Recovery covers never-started, partial-start, interrupted stream, missing payload, stale CRDT base, orphaned subagent, failed cloud consent, cancelled background process, crashed app, and restart-after-crash paths with typed codes and recovery hints.

Recovery MUST also recreate MT lifecycle/status signaling for claimed_by, blocker, missing_resource, proof_status, hbr_status, ready_for_validation state, last_recovery_event_ref, and last_runtime_status_ref before implementation resumes.

Recovery negative proof MUST cover corrupt checkpoint, EventLedger sequence gap, duplicate idempotency, missing payload ref, stale CRDT base, failed cloud consent, cancelled process, crashed app, and orphaned subagent.
HBR-INT-009 posture MUST use a shared Rust typed record with behavior_id, tier, status, reason, follow_up_ref, and evidence_ref; FR-only evidence must fail.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: Parallel swarms will fail in ways the operator and later models cannot recover.

implementation_notes: Recovery must cover PLANNED, STARTING, READY, RUNNING, WAITING, BLOCKED, DONE, FAILED, and CANCELLED lane states.
Never-started, partial-start, interrupted stream, missing payload, orphan subagent, failed cloud consent, and cancelled background process paths must have deterministic state.
Use CX-MM-006 for missing/corrupt/unrecoverable payload refs and CX-MM-009 for orphaned/unrecoverable subagent lanes.

Every observable runtime behavior must record the HBR-INT-009 three-tier outcome: Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry. Until internal_diagnostics/Palmistry ship, record DEFERRED-with-reason, never omit.
Flight Recorder/EventLedger records must include lane_id, run_id, session_id/model_session_id, wp_id, mt_id, status transitions, error code, artifact refs, and replay hint.
The project-named internal viewing tool must reconstruct lane run state from durable records alone.
Event-sourcing research maps directly to append-only EventLedger rows plus projections and snapshots; recovery must prefer replayable authority over mutable current-state shortcuts.
OpenTelemetry/Dapper patterns require trace/span/correlation propagation across all lane/process/provider boundaries, not just per-provider request ids.
Palmistry/internal_diagnostics may be deferred if absent in this worktree, but the recovery evidence schema must record WIRED, NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason, or DEFERRED-with-reason per HBR-INT-009.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: Recovery can pass happy-path tests but fail for never-started or partial-start lanes.
minimum_controls: Test never-started, partial-success, interrupted, failed, and completed lanes.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/restart_resume.rs replays from checkpoints and EventLedger sequence
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Recovery must reconstruct ModelLaneRun from PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT checkpoints and events, handle leases/orphans/cancel/restart paths, and expose three-tier diagnostic posture.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
